body:
Title: CRA Form MB479 2025 Manitoba Credits.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5007-tc/5007-tc-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5007-tc/5007-tc-25e.pdf

CRA Form MB479 2025 calculates refundable Manitoba credits. The family income section uses net income from line 23600, adds UCCB and RDSP repayments, subtracts UCCB and RDSP income, and floors negative amounts at zero; line 60890 excludes the spouse column when separate principal residences were occupied for medical reasons. The personal tax credit section gives a $195 basic credit, $113 age credits, $113 disability credits, $62 disabled dependant credits, and $26 dependent child credits, then reduces total credits by 1% of family income.

The homeowners affordability tax credit section adds school taxes line 61120 and homeowners affordability advance line 61140, prorates a $1,500 base amount by qualified days divided by 365, uses the lesser amount, and subtracts the advance. The renters affordability tax credit section uses a senior base of $903.57, a family-income reduction rate of 0.8214% capped at $328.57, 12 months in the year, and a monthly floor of $47.91. The seniors school tax rebate requires family income less than $63,500, caps line 44 at $235, subtracts 1% of family income above $40,000, and prorates by the homeowners qualified-days fraction.

The other credits section carries line 55 through any EIA or MSPD line 61255 adjustment, adds primary caregiver credit line 61260, fertility treatment credit equal to 40% of eligible medical expenses capped at $16,000, paid work experience credit line 61310, unused odour-control credit line 61340, green energy purchaser credit line 61380, green energy manufacturer credit equal to 7.5% of adjusted geothermal heat pump cost, book publishing credit line 61430, cultural industries printing credit capped at $1,100,000, community enterprise development credit capped at $27,000, employee share purchase credit capped at $27,000, teaching expense credit equal to 15% of eligible teaching supplies expenses capped at $1,000, and rental housing construction incentive credit line 80. Line 81 enters Manitoba credits on T1 line 47900.
